name: gwdatafind
documentation:
# GWDataFind

The GWDataFind data discovery client.

The GWDataFind service allows users to query for the location of
Gravitational-Wave Frame (GWF) files containing data from the current
gravitational-wave detectors.

[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/gwdatafind.svg)](http://badge.fury.io/py/gwdatafind)
[![Linux status](https://git.ligo.org/lscsoft/gwdatafind/badges/main/pipeline.svg)](https://git.ligo.org/lscsoft/gwdatafind/commits/main)
[![Windows status](https://ci.appveyor.com/api/projects/status/js6gql8960qa9pkl?svg=true)](https://ci.appveyor.com/project/duncanmmacleod/gwdatafind)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/gwdatafind.svg)](https://choosealicense.com/licenses/gpl-3.0/)
[![Documentation status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/gwdatafind/badge/?version=latest)](https://gwdatafind.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)

## Installation

The simplest installation is via `pip`:

```bash
python -m pip install gwdatafind
```

This package is also available as a Conda package:

```bash
conda install -c conda-forge gwdatafind
```

## Basic Usage

To find the URLs of all `H1_R` files for the LIGO-Hanford observatory in
a given GPS interval:

```python
>>> from gwdatafind import find_urls
>>> find_urls('H', 'H1_R', 1198800018, 1198800618)
```

To utillise connection pooling, create a `Session`:

```python
>>> from gwdatafind import (find_urls, Session)
>>> with Session() as sess:
...     for ifo in ("H", "L"):
...         urls[ifo] = find_urls(
...             ifo,
...             f"{ifo}1_R",
...             1198800018,
...             1198800618,
...             session=sess,
...         )

## On the command-line

GWDataFind can also be executed via the command-line client, for full details
run

```bash
$ python -m gwdatafind --help
```

For more documentation, see <https://gwdatafind.readthedocs.io/>.

functions.name: gwdatafind.utils.get_default_host
documentation:
Return the default host as stored in the `${GWDATAFIND_SERVER}`.

# Returns

host

: the URL of the default host

# Raises

ValueError

: if the `GWDATAFIND_SERVER` environment variable is not set

functions.name: gwdatafind.utils.validate_proxy
documentation:
Validate an X509 proxy certificate file.

:::{warning}
This method is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.
:::

This function tests that the proxy certificate is
`RFC 3820 <https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3820.txt>` compliant and is
not expired.

# Parameters

path

: the path of the X509 file on disk.

# Returns

valid

: if the certificate validiates.

# Raises

RuntimeError

: if the certificate cannot be validated.

functions.name: gwdatafind.utils.find_credential
documentation:
Locate X509 certificate and key files.

:::{warning}
This method is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.
:::

This function checks the following paths in order:

- `${X509_USER_PROXY}`
- `${X509_USER_CERT}` and `${X509_USER_KEY}`
- `/tmp/x509up_u${UID}`

# Returns

cert, key

: the paths of the certificate and key files, respectively.

# Raises

RuntimeError

: if not certificate files can be found, or if the files found on
  disk cannot be validtted.

functions.name: gwdatafind.utils.filename_metadata
documentation:
Return metadata parsed from a filename following LIGO-T050017.

# Parameters

filename

: the path name of a file

# Returns

obs

: the observatory metadata

tag

: the file tag

segment

: the GPS `[start, stop)` interval for this file

# Notes

[LIGO-T050017](https://dcc.ligo.org/LIGO-T050017) declares a
file naming convention that includes documenting the GPS start integer
and integer duration of a file, see that document for more details.

functions.name: gwdatafind.utils.file_segment
documentation:
Return the data segment for a filename following LIGO-T050017.

# Parameters

filename

: the path of a file.

# Returns

segment

: the `[start, stop)` GPS segment covered by the given file

modules.name: gwdatafind.ui
documentation:
User interface functions for GWDataFind.

These are all imported to the top-level namespace, so should be
referred to in usage as `gwdatafind.<function>` and not
`gwdatafind.ui.<function>`

functions.name: gwdatafind.ui.connect
documentation:
Open a new connection to a Datafind server.

This method will auto-select between HTTP and HTTPS based on port,
and (for HTTPS) will automatically load the necessary X509 credentials
using {func}`gwdatafind.utils.find_credential`.

:::{warning}
This method is deprecated and will be removed in a future release
:::

# Parameters

host

: the name of the datafind server to connect to; if not given will be
  taken from the `GWDATAFIND_SERVER` environment variable.

port

: the port on the server to use, if not given it will be stripped from
  the `host` name.

# Returns

connection

: a newly opened connection

functions.name: gwdatafind.ui.get_json
documentation:
Perform a GET request and return JSON.

# Parameters

args, kwargs

: all keyword arguments are passed directly to
  {meth}`igwn_auth_utils.requests.get`

# Returns

data

: the URL reponse parsed with {func}`json.loads`

# See also

igwn_auth_utils.requests.get

: for information on how the request is performed

functions.name: gwdatafind.ui._url
documentation:
Construct the full URL for a query to `host` using an API function.

# Parameters

host

: the host to query, if `None` `~gwdatafind.utils.get_default_host()`
  will be used to discover the default host.

api_func

: the function from the {mod}`gwdatafind.api` module to use in
  constructing the URL path.

args, kwargs

: any positional or keyword arguments are passed directly as
  `api_func(*args, **kwargs)`

# Returns

url

: a full URL including scheme, host, and path

functions.name: gwdatafind.ui.ping
documentation:
Ping the GWDataFind host to test for life.

# Parameters

host

: the URL/name of the GWDataFind host to query; if not given
  {func}`~gwdatafind.utils.get_default_host` will be used to
  discover the default host.

ext

: the file extension for which to search.

session

: the connection session to use; if not given, a
  {class}`igwn_auth_utils.requests.Session` will be
  created on-the-fly

token, token_audience, token_scope

: passed directly to {class}`~igwn_auth_utils.Session`, see
  {ref}`scitokens` for more details.

cert

: passed directly to {class}`~igwn_auth_utils.Session`, see
  {ref}`x509` for more details.

request_kw

: other keywords are passed to {func}`igwn_auth_utils.get`

# Raises

requests.RequestException

: if the request fails for any reason

functions.name: gwdatafind.ui.find_observatories
documentation:
Query a GWDataFind host for observatories with available data.

# Parameters

match

: restrict returned observatories to those matching a
  regular expression.

host

: the URL/name of the GWDataFind host to query; if not given
  {func}`~gwdatafind.utils.get_default_host` will be used to
  discover the default host.

ext

: the file extension for which to search.

session

: the connection session to use; if not given, a
  {class}`igwn_auth_utils.requests.Session` will be
  created on-the-fly

token, token_audience, token_scope

: passed directly to {class}`~igwn_auth_utils.Session`, see
  {ref}`scitokens` for more details.

cert

: passed directly to {class}`~igwn_auth_utils.Session`, see
  {ref}`x509` for more details.

request_kw

: other keywords are passed to {func}`igwn_auth_utils.get`

# Returns

obs

: the list of known observatories prefices (and combinations)

# Raises

requests.RequestsException

: if the request fails for any reason

# Examples

```pycon
>>> find_observatories(host="datafind.gwosc.org")
['L', 'V', 'H']
>>> find_observatories(match="H", host="datafind.gwosc.org")
['H']
```

functions.name: gwdatafind.ui.find_types
documentation:
Query a GWDataFind host for dataset types.

# Parameters

site

: single-character name of site to match; if not given
  types for all sites will be returned.

match

: regular expression to match against known types

host

: the URL/name of the GWDataFind host to query; if not given
  {func}`~gwdatafind.utils.get_default_host` will be used to
  discover the default host.

ext

: the file extension for which to search.

session

: the connection session to use; if not given, a
  {class}`~igwn_auth_utils.Session` will be
  created on-the-fly

token, token_audience, token_scope

: passed directly to {class}`~igwn_auth_utils.Session`, see
  {ref}`scitokens` for more details.

cert

: passed directly to {class}`~igwn_auth_utils.Session`, see
  {ref}`x509` for more details.

request_kw

: other keywords are passed to {func}`igwn_auth_utils.get`

# Returns

types

: list of dataset types

# Raises

requests.RequestsException

: if the request fails for any reason

# Examples

```pycon
>>> find_types(host="datafind.gwosc.org")
['H2_LOSC_4_V1', 'V1_GWOSC_O3a_16KHZ_R1', 'H1_LOSC_16_V1', 'L1_LOSC_4_V1', 'V1_GWOSC_O2_16KHZ_R1', 'V1_GWOSC_O3a_4KHZ_R1', 'L1_GWOSC_O3a_4KHZ_R1', 'L1_GWOSC_O2_16KHZ_R1', 'L1_GWOSC_O2_4KHZ_R1', 'V1_GWOSC_O2_4KHZ_R1', 'H1_LOSC_4_V1', 'H1_GWOSC_O3a_16KHZ_R1', 'H1_GWOSC_O2_16KHZ_R1', 'H1_GWOSC_O3a_4KHZ_R1', 'L1_GWOSC_O3a_16KHZ_R1', 'H1_GWOSC_O2_4KHZ_R1', 'L1_LOSC_16_V1']
>>> find_types(site='V', host="datafind.gwosc.org")
['V1_GWOSC_O3a_4KHZ_R1', 'V1_GWOSC_O3a_16KHZ_R1', 'V1_GWOSC_O2_4KHZ_R1', 'V1_GWOSC_O2_16KHZ_R1']
```

(accurate as of Nov 18 2021)

functions.name: gwdatafind.ui.find_times
documentation:
Query a GWDataFind host for times in which data are available.

# Parameters

site

: single-character name of site to match

frametype

: name of dataset to match

gpsstart

: GPS start time of query

gpsend

: GPS end time of query

match

: regular expression to match against known types

host

: the URL/name of the GWDataFind host to query; if not given
  {func}`~gwdatafind.utils.get_default_host` will be used to
  discover the default host.

ext

: the file extension for which to search.

session

: the connection session to use; if not given, a
  {class}`igwn_auth_utils.requests.Session` will be
  created on-the-fly

token, token_audience, token_scope

: passed directly to {class}`~igwn_auth_utils.Session`, see
  {ref}`scitokens` for more details.

cert

: passed directly to {class}`~igwn_auth_utils.Session`, see
  {ref}`x509` for more details.

request_kw

: other keywords are passed to {func}`igwn_auth_utils.get`

# Returns

segments

: the list of `[start, end)` intervals during which data are
  available for the relevant dataset.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> find_times(
...     "V",
...     "V1_GWOSC_O3a_4KHZ_R1",
...     gpsstart=1238249472,
...     gpsend=1239429120,
...     host="datafind.gwosc.org",
... )
[segment(1238249472, 1238417408), segment(1238421504, 1238605824), segment(1238609920, 1238827008), segment(1238839296, 1239429120)]
```

# Raises

requests.RequestsException

: if the request fails for any reason

functions.name: gwdatafind.ui.find_url
documentation:
Query a GWDataFind host for the URL of a single filename.

# Parameters

framefile

: the name of the file to match; note that only the basename of
  the file is relevant.

urltype

: URL scheme to search for

on_missing

: what to do when the requested file isn't found, one of:

  - `'error'`: raise a `RuntimeError`
  - `'warn'`: print a warning but return an empty `list`
  - `'ignore'`: return an empty `list` with no warnings

host

: the URL/name of the GWDataFind host to query; if not given
  {func}`~gwdatafind.utils.get_default_host` will be used to
  discover the default host.

session

: the connection session to use; if not given, a
  {class}`igwn_auth_utils.requests.Session` will be
  created on-the-fly

token, token_audience, token_scope

: passed directly to {class}`~igwn_auth_utils.Session`, see
  {ref}`scitokens` for more details.

cert

: passed directly to {class}`~igwn_auth_utils.Session`, see
  {ref}`x509` for more details.

request_kw

: other keywords are passed to {func}`igwn_auth_utils.get`

# Returns

urls

: a list of URLs for all instances of `filename`

# Raises

requests.RequestsException

: if the request fails for any reason

RuntimeError

: if no matching URLs are found and `on_missing="error"` was given

functions.name: gwdatafind.ui.find_latest
documentation:
Query a GWDataFind host for the latest file in a given dataset.

# Parameters

site

: single-character name of site to match

frametype

: name of dataset to match

urltype

: URL scheme to search for

on_missing

: what to do when the requested file isn't found, one of:

  - `'error'`: raise a `RuntimeError`
  - `'warn'`: print a warning but return an empty `list`
  - `'ignore'`: return an empty `list` with no warnings

host

: the URL/name of the GWDataFind host to query; if not given
  {func}`~gwdatafind.utils.get_default_host` will be used to
  discover the default host.

ext

: the file extension for which to search.

session

: the connection session to use; if not given, a
  {class}`igwn_auth_utils.requests.Session` will be
  created on-the-fly

token, token_audience, token_scope

: passed directly to {class}`~igwn_auth_utils.Session`, see
  {ref}`scitokens` for more details.

cert

: passed directly to {class}`~igwn_auth_utils.Session`, see
  {ref}`x509` for more details.

request_kw

: other keywords are passed to {func}`igwn_auth_utils.get`

# Returns

urls

: a list of URLs for the latest file found

# Raises

requests.RequestsException

: if the request fails for any reason

RuntimeError

: if no latest file is found and `on_missing="error"` was given

# Examples

```pycon
>>> find_latest('H', 'H1_GWOSC_O2_4KHZ_R1', urltype='file', host='datafind.gwosc.org'))
['file://localhost/cvmfs/gwosc.osgstorage.org/gwdata/O2/strain.4k/frame.v1/H1/1186988032/H-H1_GWOSC_O2_4KHZ_R1-1187733504-4096.gwf']
```

functions.name: gwdatafind.ui.find_urls
documentation:
Query a GWDataFind host for all URLs for a dataset in an interval.

# Parameters

site

: single-character name of site to match

frametype

: name of dataset to match

gpsstart

: GPS start time of interval

gpsend

: GPS end time of interval

match

: regular expression pattern to match URLs against

urltype

: URL scheme to search for

on_gaps

: what to do when the requested all or some of the GPS interval
  is not covereed by the dataset, one of:

  - `'error'`: raise a `RuntimeError`
  - `'warn'`: print a warning but return all available URLs
  - `'ignore'`: return the list of URLs with no warnings

host

: the URL/name of the GWDataFind host to query; if not given
  {func}`~gwdatafind.utils.get_default_host` will be used to
  discover the default host.

ext

: the file extension for which to search.

session

: the connection session to use; if not given, a
  {class}`igwn_auth_utils.requests.Session` will be
  created on-the-fly

token, token_audience, token_scope

: passed directly to {class}`~igwn_auth_utils.Session`, see
  {ref}`scitokens` for more details.

cert

: passed directly to {class}`~igwn_auth_utils.Session`, see
  {ref}`x509` for more details.

request_kw

: other keywords are passed to {func}`igwn_auth_utils.get`

# Raises

requests.RequestsException

: if the request fails for any reason

RuntimeError

: if gaps in the dataset are found and `on_gaps="error"` was given

classes.name: gwdatafind.http.HTTPConnection

methods.name: gwdatafind.http.HTTPConnection.__init__
documentation: Create a new connection.

methods.name: gwdatafind.http.HTTPConnection._request_response
documentation:
Internal method to perform request and verify reponse.

# Parameters

method

: name of the method to use (e.g. `'GET'`).

url

: remote URL to query.

kwargs

: other keyword arguments are passed to
  {meth}`http.client.HTTPConnection.request`.

# Returns

response

: reponse from server query

# Raises

RuntimeError

: if query is unsuccessful

methods.name: gwdatafind.http.HTTPConnection.get_json
documentation:
Perform a 'GET' request and return the decode the result as JSON

# Parameters

url

: remote URL to query

kwargs

: other keyword arguments are passed to
  {meth}`HTTPConnection._request_response`

# Returns

data

: JSON decoded using {func}`json.loads`

methods.name: gwdatafind.http.HTTPConnection.get_urls
documentation:
Perform a 'GET' request and return a list of URLs.

# Parameters

url

: remote URL to query

scheme

: the URL scheme to match, default: `None`

on_missing

: how to handle an empty (but successful) response, one of

  - `'ignore'`: do nothing, return empty `list`
  - `'warn'`: print warning, return empty `list`
  - `'raise'`: raise `RuntimeError`

kwargs

: other keyword arguments are passed to
  {meth}`HTTPConnection.get_json`

# Returns

urls

: a list of file paths as returned from the server.

methods.name: gwdatafind.http.HTTPConnection.ping
documentation:
Ping the LDR host to test for life.

Raises
------
RuntimeError
    if the ping fails
-- supported interactions -----------------

methods.name: gwdatafind.http.HTTPConnection.find_observatories
documentation:
Query the LDR host for observatories.

# Parameters

match

: restrict returned observatories to those matching a
  regular expression.

# Returns

obs

: the list of known osbervatory prefices (and combinations)

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from gwdatafind import connect
>>> conn = connect()
>>> conn.find_observatories()
['AGHLT', 'G', 'GHLTV', 'GHLV', 'GHT', 'H', 'HL', 'HLT',
 'L', 'T', 'V', 'Z']
>>> conn.find_observatories("H")
['H', 'HL', 'HLT']
```

methods.name: gwdatafind.http.HTTPConnection.find_types
documentation:
Query the LDR host for frame types.

# Parameters

site

: single-character name of site to match

match

: regular expression to match against known types

# Returns

types

: list of frame types

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from gwdatafind import connect
>>> conn = connect()
>>> conn.find_types("L", "RDS")
['L1_RDS_C01_LX',
 'L1_RDS_C02_LX',
 'L1_RDS_C03_L2',
 'L1_RDS_R_L1',
 'L1_RDS_R_L3',
 'L1_RDS_R_L4',
 'PEM_RDS_A6',
 'RDS_R_L1',
 'RDS_R_L2',
 'RDS_R_L3',
 'TESTPEM_RDS_A6']
```

methods.name: gwdatafind.http.HTTPConnection.find_times
documentation:
Query the LDR for times for which files are avaliable.

Parameters
----------
site : `str`
    single-character name of site to match

frametype : `str`
    name of frametype to match

gpsstart : `int`
    GPS start time of query

gpsend : `int`
    GPS end time of query

Returns
-------
segments : `ligo.segments.segmentlist`
    the list of `[start, stop)` intervals for which files are
    available.

methods.name: gwdatafind.http.HTTPConnection.find_url
documentation:
Query the LDR host for a single filename.

# Parameters

framefile

: base name of file to match

urltype

: file scheme to search for, one of `'file'`, `'gsiftp'`, or
  `None`, default: 'file'

on_missing

: what to do when the requested file isn't found, one of:

  - `'warn'`: print a warning (default),
  - `'error'`: raise a `RuntimeError`, or
  - `'ignore'`: do nothing

# Returns

urls

: a list of structured file paths for all instances of `filename`.

methods.name: gwdatafind.http.HTTPConnection.find_frame
documentation: DEPRECATED, use {meth}`~HTTPConnection.find_url` instead.

methods.name: gwdatafind.http.HTTPConnection.find_latest
documentation:
Query for the most recent file of a given type.

# Parameters

site

: single-character name of site to match

frametype

: name of frametype to match

urltype

: file scheme to search for, one of 'file', 'gsiftp', or
  `None`, default: 'file'

on_missing

: what to do when the requested frame isn't found, one of:

  - `'warn'` print a warning (default), or
  - `'error'`: raise a `RuntimeError`, or
  - `'ignore'`: do nothing

# Returns

latest

: the URLs of the latest file found (all file types)

# Raises

RuntimeError

: if no frames are found

methods.name: gwdatafind.http.HTTPConnection.find_urls
documentation:
Find all files of the given type in the \[start, end) GPS interval.

site

: single-character name of site to match

frametype

: name of frametype to match

gpsstart

: integer GPS start time of query

gpsend

: integer GPS end time of query

match

: regular expression to match against

urltype

: file scheme to search for, one of 'file', 'gsiftp', or
  `None`, default: 'file'

on_gaps

: what to do when the requested frame isn't found, one of:

  - `'warn'` print a warning (default), or
  - `'error'`: raise a `RuntimeError`, or
  - `'ignore'`: do nothing

# Returns

cache

: the list of discovered file URLs

methods.name: gwdatafind.http.HTTPConnection.find_frame_urls
documentation: DEPRECATED, use {meth}`~HTTPConnection.find_urls` instead.

Connect to a GWDataFind host using HTTP.

:::{warning}
This class is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.
Use the functions from the {ref}`gwdatafind-top-api` instead.
:::

# Parameters

host

: the name of the server with which to connect.

port

: the port on which to connect.

kwargs

: other keywords are passed directly to `http.client.HTTPConnection`

classes.name: gwdatafind.http.HTTPSConnection

methods.name: gwdatafind.http.HTTPSConnection.__init__
documentation: Create a new connection.

Connect to a GWDataFind host using HTTPS.

:::{warning}
This class is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.
Use the functions from the {ref}`gwdatafind-top-api` instead.
:::

This requires a valid X509 credential registered with the remote host.

# Parameters

host

: the name of the server with which to connect.

port

: the port on which to connect.

kwargs

: other keywords are passed directly to `http.client.HTTPSConnection`

modules.name: gwdatafind
documentation:
The client library for the GWDataFind service.

The GWDataFind service allows users to query for the location of
Gravitational-Wave data files containing data associated with
gravitational-wave detectors.

This package provides a number of functions to make requests to
a GWDataFind server with authorization credential handling.

# Quick-start

The following convenience functions are provided to perform single queries
without a persistent connection:

```
.. currentmodule:: gwdatafind
```

```
.. autosummary::
    :nosignatures:

    ping
    find_observatories
    find_types
    find_times
    find_url
    find_urls
    find_latest
```

For example:

```pycon
>>> from gwdatafind import find_urls
>>> urls = find_urls("L", "L1_GWOSC_O2_4KHZ_R1", 1187008880, 1187008884,
...                  host="datafind.gwosc.org")
>>> print(urls)
['file://localhost/cvmfs/gwosc.osgstorage.org/gwdata/O2/strain.4k/frame.v1/L1/1186988032/L-L1_GWOSC_O2_4KHZ_R1-1187008512-4096.gwf']
```

Additionally, one can create a `requests.Session` to reuse a
connection to a server.

For example:

```pycon
>>> from gwdatafind import (find_observatories, find_urls, Session)
>>> with Session() as sess:
...     obs = find_observatories(
...               host="datafind.gwosc.org",
...               session=sess,
...     )
...     print(obs)
...     urls = {}
...     for ifo in obs:
...         urls[ifo] = find_urls(
...             ifo,
...             f"{ifo}1_GWOSC_O2_4KHZ_R1",
...             1187008880,
...             1187008884,
...             host="datafind.gwosc.org",
...             session=sess,
...         )
...     print(urls)
['H', 'V', 'L']
{'H': ['file://localhost/cvmfs/gwosc.osgstorage.org/gwdata/O2/strain.4k/frame.v1/H1/1186988032/H-H1_GWOSC_O2_4KHZ_R1-1187008512-4096.gwf'],
 'V': ['file://localhost/cvmfs/gwosc.osgstorage.org/gwdata/O2/strain.4k/frame.v1/V1/1186988032/V-V1_GWOSC_O2_4KHZ_R1-1187008512-4096.gwf'],
 'L': ['file://localhost/cvmfs/gwosc.osgstorage.org/gwdata/O2/strain.4k/frame.v1/L1/1186988032/L-L1_GWOSC_O2_4KHZ_R1-1187008512-4096.gwf']}
```
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
Copyright (C) Cardiff University (2017-2022)

documentation:
Convert a list of URLs into a LAL cache.

Returns
-------
cache : `list` of `LalCacheEntry`

functions.name: gwdatafind.io.omega_cache
documentation:
Convert a list of `LalCacheEntry` into a list of `OmegaCacheEntry`.

Returns
-------
cache : `list` of `OmegaCacheEntry`

functions.name: gwdatafind.io.format_cache
documentation:
Format a list of `LalCacheEntry` into a different format.

Valid formats:

- `omega` - return a list of Omega-format cache entries

classes.name: gwdatafind.io.LalCacheEntry

methods.name: gwdatafind.io.LalCacheEntry.from_url
documentation: Create a new `LalCacheEntry` from a URL that follows LIGO-T050017.

Simplified version of `lal.utils.CacheEntry`.

This is provided so that we don't have to depend on lalsuite.

classes.name: gwdatafind.io.OmegaCacheEntry
documentation:
CacheEntry for an omega-style cache.

Omega-style cache files contain one entry per contiguous directory of
the form:

    <obs> <tag> <dir-start> <dir-end> <file-duration> <directory>

functions.name: gwdatafind.__main__.postprocess_cache
documentation:
Post-process a cache produced from a DataFind query.

This function checks for gaps in the file coverage, prints the cache
in the requested format, then prints gaps to stderr if requested.

methods.name: gwdatafind.__main__.DataFindArgumentParser.sanity_check
documentation:
Sanity check parsed command line options.

If any problems are found `argparse.ArgumentParser.error` is called,
which in turn calls {func}`sys.exit`.

# Parameters

namespace

: the output of the command-line parsing

Custom `~argparse.ArgumentParser` for GWDataFind.

Mainly to handle the legacy mutually-exclusive optional arguments...
